title: Pell Norm PASS: 6 general witnesses + off-chain Pell directions
_fixture_purpose: General witnesses for I=-(x²-2y²) across diverse directions — both on and off the Pell boundary chain. The identity holds for ALL QA directions, not just the boundary ones with |P|=1. For example (3,2): x=1,y=2, P=1-8=-7, I=7; (4,1): x=3,y=1, P=9-2=7, I=-7. The Pell norm P(x,y)=x²-2y² can take any integer value, and |I|=|P| measures how far the direction is from the parabola boundary.

general_theorem.identity: For all QA directions (d,e): I = C-F = -(x²-2y²) where x=d-e, y=e
general_theorem.boundary_interpretation: |P|=|x²-2y²| = distance from parabola boundary in Pell units
general_theorem.pell_chain: P=±1 directions form the M_B spine; P=±7 directions include (3,2),(4,1),(8,3),(9,4) — next tier

pell_norm_levels._note: Pell norm |P| = distance from parabola boundary; directions with same |P| are 'equidistant' from boundary
